summary: The S&P 500 breached 7,000 on April 15, driven by a rare alignment of cooling geopolitical tensions and a blockbuster start to Q1 earnings. Goldman Sachs kicked off with a massive beat, posting $5.33 billion in equities trading revenue, setting a bullish tone for financials. Tariff exposure guidance is now the operative variable — companies reporting record Q1 results are selling off on cautious Q2 outlooks tied to elevated tariff costs.
tweet: S&P 500 hit 7,000 on cooling Iran tensions and bank earnings beats, but tariff guidance is now the market's real scorecard — beats mean nothing without clean Q2 outlooks.
